title: pulmonaria angustifolia subsp. longifolia
intro: Pulmonaria angustifolia subsp. longifolia, also known as Lungwort, is a type of flowering plant in the borage family (Boraginaceae) that is native to Europe. Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and expectorant properties, which help to relieve respiratory issues and soothe coughs. The plant is rich in saponins, flavonoids, and other compounds that provide various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving immune function, as well as acting as an expectorant to help clear mucus from the lungs. Pulmonaria angustifolia subsp. longifolia is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion for its medicinal uses, and can also be used topically as a poultice or salve to help soothe skin irritations and wounds.
